name: Servbank, National Association
description_short: FDIC-insured national bank offering personal and commercial banking, mortgage subservicing, and specialized HOA banking solutions with a focus on community bank excellence.
description_long:
Servbank, National Association is a federally chartered bank that operates as both a direct banking institution and a specialized subservicer for mortgage lending operations. The company recently completed an acquisition of Iroquois Federal, expanding its footprint in community banking. With FDIC insurance backing and recognition from S&P Global as one of the best-performing community banks, Servbank positions itself as a relationship-driven banking partner.

Servbank offers three primary service lines: personal banking with checking and savings accounts, online and mobile platforms, and nationwide ATM access; commercial and business banking featuring customized lending solutions, fraud prevention, treasury management, and dedicated relationship managers; and specialized homeowners association (HOA) banking with tailored deposit solutions, lines of credit with terms up to 20 years, and reserve account management through ICS and CDARS programs. Additionally, the bank operates a significant mortgage subservicing division serving loan servicers and lenders with technology-enabled loan servicing, compliance support, and customer care operations.

Servbank differentiates itself through several distinctive offerings: recognition by Fannie Mae's STAR program, expertise in community banking combined with enterprise-scale subservicing capabilities, and specific focus on underserved niches like HOA banking with features like prepayment penalty waivers. The company emphasizes a "human-first approach" alongside technology adoption and has earned leadership recognition, including naming of its CFO as a 2026 HousingWire Finance Leader.

As a traditional bank, Servbank serves established customers seeking FDIC-insured deposit accounts and businesses needing lending relationships rather than consumers seeking credit repair, fast cash, or debt relief. The main caveat is that this is primarily a commercial and institutional banking platform—individual consumers should expect a traditional bank experience focused on account management and lending rather than consumer credit products or lending to subprime borrowers. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Commercial lending and customized loan solutions
Fraud prevention and treasury management services
Fund banking and subscription line financing
HOA lines of credit with up to 20-year terms
Insured Cash Sweep (ICS) and CDARS programs for deposit protection
Competitive money market accounts for HOA reserves
Mortgage loan subservicing with technology platform
Compliance and risk management support for subservicing clients
Business relationship management and dedicated account managers
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Recognized by S&P Global as one of best-performing community banks
Nationwide ATM access with 24/7 online and mobile banking availability
HOA-specific banking with competitive money market rates and 20-year line of credit terms
Fannie Mae STAR program recognition for mortgage subservicing excellence
No prepayment penalties on HOA lines of credit for added flexibility
Dedicated relationship managers for personalized commercial banking support
cons: As a traditional bank, likely has higher minimum balance requirements typical of community banks
Limited consumer credit products compared to large retail banks—focused on deposits and lending relationships
Recent acquisition integration (Iroquois Federal) may create operational or service transition uncertainties
best_for: Small to mid-sized business owners seeking customized lending and treasury management services
Homeowners association boards and property management companies needing specialized banking solutions
Mortgage lenders and servicers requiring compliance-focused subservicing partnerships
Community-focused depositors preferring a relationship-driven bank over large national institutions
diagnosis: Servbank is best for established small-to-mid-size businesses, mortgage lenders/servicers, and HOA boards seeking FDIC-insured banking from a community bank recognized for performance and compliance expertise. The main caveat is that this is a traditional institutional and commercial bank, not a consumer lender for credit-challenged individuals or a source of alternative financial products.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: FDIC-insured national bank offering personal and commercial banking, mortgage subservicing, and HOA solutions.
